test_cases.input_summary: 'What Your Doctor Didn't Tell You' — 55-minute health podcast. Host + one guest format, personal opening monologue (5 mins), heavy listener questions (15-20 mins), casual blunt tone. Target: national public radio, 11am weekday, 28 minutes.
test_cases.output_summary: Full adaptation brief with comparison table and 5 specific recommendations. Monologue-to-tease recommendation is specific and explains the why. Listener questions section preserves the podcast's engagement identity in reduced form. Compliance flags are Ofcom-specific and accurate. Sample structure maps to 28-minute slot with 2 minutes flex.
test_cases.notes: Strong professionalism and coherence. The document correctly distinguishes which podcast qualities to preserve vs change — a common failure in adaptation briefs. Relevance slightly marked: compliance flags included one Ofcom section reference that was not precisely the right provision for health content — flagged and verified before use. Actionability: used as the basis for a broadcaster meeting with minor additions.

test_cases.input_summary: True crime narrative podcast, 6-part series, 45 minutes per episode. Highly produced with music beds and cinematic audio design. Target: commercial radio evening strand, 35 minutes per episode. US-based broadcaster (FCC context).
test_cases.output_summary: Adaptation correctly identifies FCC rather than Ofcom standards. Music beds flagged as a production asset that can be largely preserved but must meet DMCA/broadcast licensing requirements. Duration reduction recommendations focus on narrative tightening rather than structural removal. Ad break positions integrated into the adapted structure.
test_cases.notes: Good relevance — the FCC context was correctly applied. Music licensing compliance note was appropriate but brief; a producer would need more detail before clearance. Coherence: one recommendation section ran across two related changes without clearly separating them into distinct recommendations.

test_cases.input_summary: Tech explainer podcast (weekly, 25 minutes) — already close to broadcast length. Target: regional public radio, mid-morning. Audience shift: from tech enthusiasts to general adult listener. Duration: 22 minutes with a news break at 15 minutes.
test_cases.output_summary: Adaptation correctly identifies the primary challenge as audience rather than duration — the tech-enthusiast register needs recalibration for a general listener. Comparison table focuses on expertise assumption differences. The news break is correctly integrated as a fixed point in the adapted structure. Minimal structural disruption needed.
test_cases.notes: The reframe from 'duration problem' to 'audience problem' is the correct editorial diagnosis and shows the skill is doing genuine analysis, not just applying a template. Actionability high — the producer adapted the first episode using this document directly. Consistency: minor — one recommendation referenced 'jargon' without defining which terms were the issue, which required a follow-up conversation.
